meta.title: Cross-Investigation Pattern Analysis: Obras Publicas x Finanzas Politicas
meta.methodology: Traversed SAME_ENTITY and MAYBE_SAME_AS edges between obras-publicas Contractor nodes and finanzas-politicas graph entities (Company, CompanyOfficer, GovernmentAppointment, Donor, AssetDeclaration, Politician). Searched for pay-to-play, revolving door, offshore trail, asset declaration, and politician-contractor patterns.

revolving_door.description: Company officers who held government appointments AND whose companies received public works contracts. The strongest corruption signal in this analysis.

top_cases.contractor: CORREO OFICIAL DE LA REPUBLICA ARGENTINA SA
top_cases.company: CORREO OFICIAL DE LA REPUBLICA ARGENTINA

officers_in_government.name: Catalan, Lisandro
officers_in_government.ministry: Ministerio de Justicia y Derechos Humanos

top_cases.risk_assessment: Highest value revolving door case. State-owned postal company with 5 officers simultaneously in senior government roles across multiple ministries. $10.47B in contracts makes this the single largest cross-ref finding.

top_cases.contractor: PROVINCIA ASEGURADORA DE RIESGOS DEL TABAJO SA
top_cases.company: PROVINCIA ASEGURADORA DE RIESGOS DEL TRABAJO
top_cases.risk_assessment: Insurance regulator (Vice Superintendente de Seguros) serving as company officer of insurer receiving $1.43B in state contracts. Classic regulatory capture.
Multinational pharmaceutical company with government appointee on board. $954M in contracts while officer served at production ministry.

top_cases.company: EMPRESA ARGENTINA DE SOLUCIONES SATELITALES S.A - ARSAT -
top_cases.risk_assessment: State satellite company with 22 officers in government. As a state enterprise this is expected, but the volume of contracts ($694M) alongside such deep government penetration warrants scrutiny of awarding processes.

contractor_asset_declarations.description: Contractors matched to DDJJ (declaraciones juradas / sworn asset declarations) via SAME_ENTITY links. These individuals both hold public contracts and have filed asset declarations, enabling wealth-contract correlation.

cases.note: Multiple DDJJ filings suggest multi-year government role. Cross-reference declared assets vs contract income.
7 annual asset declarations indicates sustained government employment. Contract value relatively low.

politician_contractor_chains.description: Politicians linked to contractors via SAME_ENTITY/MAYBE_SAME_AS chains (1-3 hops).

cases.note: Name match — likely same person acting as both legislator and contractor. Low value but pattern of dual roles.

pattern_gaps.pay_to_play: No direct Contractor->Company->Officer->Donor->CampaignDonation chains found. The 8 Donor cross-ref links suggest partial connections exist but the full chain does not complete through current entity resolution.
pattern_gaps.offshore: No offshore trails detected. Contractor officers do not currently link to OffshoreOfficer/OffshoreEntity nodes. May require deeper entity resolution or name fuzzy matching.
pattern_gaps.bribery_cross_refs: No BriberyCase entities cross-referenced to finanzas-politicas graph. BriberyCase nodes may lack CASE_INVOLVES relationships or the involved entities have not been resolved.
pattern_gaps.legislative_votes: No legislation matching obra/vial/infraestructura/construccion keywords found with associated votes. Legislative data may not include public works legislation, or node labels differ.

next_steps: Deepen entity resolution between Donor and CompanyOfficer nodes using fuzzy name matching to close pay-to-play chains
Cross-reference ARSAT and Correo Oficial contract awarding dates with officer appointment dates to detect temporal conflicts of interest
Enrich offshore entity resolution with CUIT-based lookups against Panama/Paradise Papers data
Build temporal analysis: when did revolving door officers enter/exit government vs when contracts were awarded
Investigate Guillermo Pedro Plate (Vice Superintendente de Seguros -> Provincia Aseguradora) as highest-signal regulatory capture case
qwen_analysis: LLM server unreachable at time of analysis. Manual analysis: The revolving door pattern is the dominant cross-investigation signal, with 13 GovernmentAppointment links concentrating in state enterprises (Correo Oficial, ARSAT) and regulated industries (insurance via Provincia Aseguradora, pharma via Abbvie). The $10.47B Correo Oficial cluster represents systemic state-enterprise capture rather than classic corruption, but the Provincia Aseguradora case (regulator-as-officer) is a textbook regulatory capture signal.
